Preterit.turoyo: Azzé lu+ḥəmmā̀m, ḥəfle ŭ mašəġle, lwəšle aǧ+ǧulayḏe am+mašiġe, aṯi. Səmle, lo+səmle, lo+lawəlle. lawəlle = lawәn ele
translations:
He
			went to the baths, washed himself and cleaned himself, put on
			clean clothes, came back. No matter what he did, they did not want
			to stay near him. (Mor Boses Mor Shushan)

He
			went to the baths, washed himself and cleaned himself, put on
			clean clothes, came back. No matter what he did, they did not want
			to stay near him. (Mor Boses Mor Shushan)

‘Why
			won’t you come near me? I have washed myself’. (Mor Boses Mor
			Shushan)

‘Why
			won’t you come near me? I have washed myself’. (Mor Boses Mor
			Shushan)

Preterit.turoyo: latimi b-ʕIwardo, w-mlawalle u=qašo, w-maḥatte b-u=gabo, šămali d-i=ʕito b-u=qawro, w fayəš tamo mlawalle u=mafəryono w-maḥatte b-u=qawro tamo
translations:
aben
			dem Pfarrer das Grabgeleit und legten ihn an der Nordseite der
			Kirche ins Grab, und dort blieb er

‘Sie gaben sie dem Erzbischof das Grabgeleite und legten ihn
			dort ins Grab’; 35/70

wenn
			einer stirbt, geben ihm die Pfarrer das Grabgeleite

Infinitive.turoyo: me-tamo mšareli kṯuli a=kṯowe d-a=ṣlawoṯe d-i=ʕito, yaʕni fanqiyoṯe, w ḥusoye, w a=kṯowe ḥrene xd-u=ʕmoḏo w d-u=beroxo, w d-u=lewoyo, w mgalyune
translations:
From
			there I began to copy ecclesiastical prayer books that are the
			Psalms, prayer books and other books such as books on christening,
			wedding, funeral ceremonies, and the Gospel
